results.code:
if ARGV.length != 1
  puts "Usage: ruby script.rb filename"
  exit(1)
end

filename = ARGV[0]

begin
  numbers_str = File.read(filename)
  numbers = numbers_str.split(',').map(&:to_i)
rescue Errno::ENOENT
  puts "File not found: #{filename}"
  exit(1)
rescue => e
  puts "Error reading file: #{e.message}"
  exit(1)
end

unique_numbers = numbers.uniq
unique_numbers.sort! { |a, b| b <=> a }
result = unique_numbers.join(',')

puts result

results.name: ruby_hash_group
results.code:
def group_words(filename)
  word_hash = {}
  File.foreach(filename).each do |line|
    word = line.strip
    if word.empty? || !word[0..0].is_alphabetical
      next
    end
    lower_letter = word[0..0].downcase
    if word_hash[lower_letter] == nil
      word_hash[lower_letter] = [word]
    else
      word_hash[lower_letter] << word
    end
  end

  result = {}
  word_hash.sort.each do |letter, words|
    result[letter] = words.join(',')
  end

  result
end

if __FILE__ == $0
  if ARGV.length != 1
    puts "Usage: ruby script.rb <filename>"
    exit(1)
  end

  filename = ARGV[0]
  grouped_words = group_words(filename)
  grouped_words.each do |letter, words|
    puts "#{letter}: #{words}"
  end
end

results.name: ruby_class_stack
results.code:
class Stack
  def initialize
    @items = []
  end

  def push(item)
    @items.push(item)
  end

  def pop
    return @items.pop if !@items.empty?
    nil
  end

  def peek
    return @items[-1] if !@items.empty?
    nil
  end
end

# Create a Stack instance
my_stack = Stack.new

# Push the numbers 1, 2, and 3 in that order
my_stack.push(1)
my_stack.push(2)
my_stack.push(3)

# Call peek and print its result
puts my_stack.peek

# Call pop and print its result
puts my_stack.pop

# Call peek again and print its result
puts my_stack.peek
